name: Pico del Fraile y Txarlazo — circular trailrunning por el Monte Santiago
region_intro: Jogging around Urduña-Orduña offers access to a diverse natural environment, situated in a wide valley protected by the Sierra Sálvada mountain range. This region features a backdrop of rocky walls, expansive meadows, and forests, providing varied terrain for running. The area is recognized for its ecological significance, forming part of the Natura 2000 network, ensuring a pristine setting for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Urduña-Orduña The most popular jogging route is Pico del Fraile y Txarlazo — circular trailrunning por el Monte Santiago, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging circular trailrunning experience through Monte Santiago. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico del Fraile – Txarlazo Summit and Monument loop from Orduña, a difficult 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route provides extensive views from the Txarlazo Summit and passes a significant monument. Local runners also love the Txarlazo loop from Orduña, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through the Sierra Sálvada foothills, often completed in about 1 hour 43 minutes. Jogging around Urduña-Orduña is defined by the majestic Sierra Sálvada mountain range, the impressive Salto del Nervión waterfall, and a network of diverse trails that traverse forests, meadows, and rocky peaks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from gentle valley paths to more demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Urduña-Orduña are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Urduña-Orduña's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Urduña-Orduña?
A: Urduña-Orduña offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 40 trails available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from easy paths to more challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes around Urduña-Orduña?
A: The terrain around Urduña-Orduña is incredibly diverse. You'll find gentle paths through the valley's meadows and crop fields, as well as more strenuous trails ascending the slopes of the magnificent &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Sálvada&lt;/strong&gt; mountain range. Expect a mix of wooded areas, rocky sections, and open vistas, providing a varied and engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in the area?
A: While many routes in Urduña-Orduña involve significant elevation, there are easier paths available, particularly in the valley floor, that can be suitable for families. These trails offer a gentler experience through meadows and forests. We recommend checking the difficulty ratings on komoot for specific routes to find the best fit for your family.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Urduña-Orduña?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Urduña-Orduña are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy a run with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to be mindful of local regulations and other trail users. Remember to carry water for both yourself and your dog.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while jogging?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter breathtaking sights like the &lt;strong&gt;Salto del Nervión&lt;/strong&gt;, the largest waterfall on the Iberian Peninsula, particularly impressive during rainy seasons. Many routes offer stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, and you might pass by other notable peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079416" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txarlazo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3361908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tologorri&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its diverse birdlife, including griffon vultures, especially in the Sierra Sálvada.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Urduña-Orduña?
A: Yes, Urduña-Orduña features several excellent circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20161095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Fraile – Txarlazo Summit and Monument loop from Orduña&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txarlazo loop from Orduña&lt;/a&gt; takes you through the Sierra Sálvada foothills. These loops provide a great way to experience the diverse landscapes without retracing your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Urduña-Orduña?
A: Urduña-Orduña is beautiful year-round, but the best time for running often depends on your preference. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery with changing foliage. The &lt;strong&gt;Salto del Nervión&lt;/strong&gt; waterfall is most spectacular during the rainy season or snowmelt (typically winter and spring). Summers can be warm, so early morning or late afternoon runs are recommended. Winters can be cold, especially at higher elevations, but offer crisp air and unique views.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. Many popular starting points for routes into the Sierra Sálvada or towards Monte Santiago will have designated parking areas. For routes starting directly from Orduña, you can often find parking within the town or at its outskirts. It's always a good idea to check the route details on komoot for specific parking recommendations.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Urduña-Orduña?
A: The running routes in Urduña-Orduña are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners frequently praise the stunning natural environment, particularly the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra Sálvada and the impressive views. The variety of terrain, from lush meadows to challenging mountain paths, is also a big draw, offering something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra Sálvada mountain range?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Sálvada&lt;/strong&gt; mountain range is a dominant feature of the landscape, and many running routes provide spectacular views of its formidable natural wall. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058732660" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Fraile y Txarlazo — circular trailrunning por el Monte Santiago&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20160975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Txarlazo loop from Orduña&lt;/a&gt; offer panoramic vistas from its peaks and slopes, allowing you to fully appreciate this impressive range.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners?
A: While many routes in the region are rated as moderate to difficult due to the mountainous terrain, there are some easier options, particularly those that stick to the valley floor or lower elevations. These paths offer a gentler introduction to running in Urduña-Orduña, allowing beginners to enjoy the scenery without overly challenging ascents. You can filter routes by difficulty on komoot to find suitable options.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while running in Urduña-Orduña?
A: Yes, the region is part of the Natura 2000 network and is ecologically significant, making it an excellent area for wildlife spotting. The Sierra Sálvada and Monte Santiago are important habitats for various bird species, including griffon vultures, Egyptian vultures, and peregrine falcons. Keep an eye out for these magnificent birds soaring above the cliffs, especially around viewpoints.
